title: Disability Access Barriers To Health And Education In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring disability access barriers to health and education services in LMICs, linking disability-prevalence surveys, facility-accessibility data, and school-enrollment data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: WHO estimates 1.3 billion people — 16 percent of the global population — live with significant disability, with 80 percent in LMICs. The Washington Group questions in DHS and census surveys now enable disability-disaggregated analysis. But facility-accessibility audits and school-enrollment data are rarely linked to disability data in ways that reveal geographic access gaps.

success_metrics.target: Every access-gap estimate links disability-prevalence, facility, and school data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
